She highlighted the killing of civilians and expressed her unwillingness to be associated with the commission any longer.

topStories.description: At the interbank foreign exchange, the local unit opened at 92.33 and kept losing ground to hit a fresh intra-day low of 92.47 against the US dollar. It eventually settled at its lifetime low of 92.45...

topStories.title: NDTV Speaks To Ukraine's Drone Pilots: Why US Needs Their Shahed Hunters Now
topStories.description: Iran Israel War: The Iranian-designed Shahed-136 drone has become one of the most recognisable weapons of the modern battlefield.

topStories.title: After Trump, Netanyahu issues 'no life insurance' warning to Mojtaba Khamenei: 'We’re not waiting'
topStories.description: Israeli Prime Minister Benjamin Netanyahu warned Iran's new supreme leader, Ayotallah Mojtaba Khamenei, that Jerusalem is ‘initiating, attacking’. This follows US President Donald Trump's warning of potential elimination if Mojtaba does not comply with demands regarding Tehran's nuclear programme.

topStories.title: 4 crew aboard refueling aircraft crashed in Iraq killed, says US Central Command, denies hostile or friendly fire
topStories.description: Four crew members of a KC-135 refueling aircraft have died following its crash in western Iraq. The US military stated the loss was not due to hostile or friendly fire, as investigations continue amidst heightened military activity in the region.

dailyNews.title: Elderly man, 66, dies after 2-hour wait in LPG cylinder queue amid supply shortage: Report
dailyNews.description: A 66-year-old man in Punjab's Barnala district allegedly died of a heart attack while waiting for an LPG cylinder for 2 hours amid supply shortages due to the Middle East conflict.  A Congress leader blamed the government for the tragic circumstances.

dailyNews.title: Metal stocks slide up to 7% as dollar hits 4-month high; NALCO, Hindustan Copper lead fall
dailyNews.description: Metal stocks, including NALCO and Hindustan Copper fell sharply on Friday as a surging US Dollar Index and ongoing Iran–Israel/US conflict dented demand. Analysts advise caution, noting that global commodity prices and infrastructure trends will shape near-term metal sector performance.

dailyNews.description: The 32 sailors who were rescued by Sri Lanka's navy will remain in the island nation, said the Foreign Ministry.

dailyNews.title: Iran war disrupts global oil trade as Trump administration prepares to escort ships
dailyNews.description: Iran's relentless attacks on Gulf states and infrastructure appear to be overshadowing interventions by the U.S. and its allies aimed at easing energy prices.

dailyNews.title: U.S. launches new investigations into 60 countries as it fights to restore tariffs
dailyNews.description: The Trump administration has launched investigations into dozens of countries accused of failing to crack down on forced labor, flexing a law that lets the federal government impose tariffs.

dailyNews.title: Trump administration allows purchase of Russian oil already at sea
dailyNews.description: The U.S. is temporarily allowing the purchase of Russian oil that's already at sea, in the Trump administration's latest move to loosen sanctions on Russia's oil industry as the world grapples with high oil prices.

dailyNews.title: Experts, reports on the Strait of Hormuz and gas prices
dailyNews.description: Ship traffic in the Strait of Hormuz was mostly stopped amid the war in Iran on Thursday, which contributed to rising oil prices. The waterway typically carries around 20% of the world's supply of oil. On Wednesday, President Trump ordered the release of 172 million barrels of oil from emergency reserves. In a statement attributed to Iran's new Supreme Leader, Ayatollah Mojtaba Khamenei, he said Iran should keep putting pressure on the critical chokepoint.

dailyNews.title: Cuba to release 51 people from prison in surprise move amid Trump pressure
dailyNews.description: Cuba's government says it will release 51 people from prisons, in an unexpected move that comes as the Trump administration puts immense pressure on the country.

dailyNews.title: A Weakened Iran Hits Back by Strangling the Vital Strait of Hormuz
dailyNews.description: The threats to shipping in the Strait of Hormuz are complicating President Trump’s calculations about how and when to end the war.

dailyNews.title: Oil Prices Rise Despite Trump’s Decision to Lift Russia Sanctions
dailyNews.description: After surging about 10 percent on Thursday, oil prices had little reaction to the decision by President Trump to waive sanctions on the sale of some Russian crude.

dailyNews.title: Trump Removes Sanctions on Russian Oil, and Chatbots Want Your Health Records
dailyNews.description: Plus, the Friday news quiz.

dailyNews.title: Joaquin Castro Is on a Quest to Get Detained Immigrants Released
dailyNews.description: The Texas Democrat has used his perch in Congress to highlight sympathetic cases in his push to free detainees and call attention to the cruel consequences of President Trump’s immigration agenda.

dailyNews.title: When a President Gets Addicted to Regime Change
dailyNews.description: Venezuela gave Trump a taste of success. This isn’t the first time an American president has gotten hooked on overthrowing foreign governments.

dailyNews.title: With Disputed Legal Maneuver, Trump Tries to Set Policy Without Legislation
dailyNews.description: By suing Republican states and making sharp reversals in old cases, the Trump administration is using courts to fast-track major shifts in policy.

dailyNews.title: Trump Targets Forced Labor in Global Tariff Scheme
dailyNews.description: The Trump administration began a trade investigation Thursday into whether dozens of countries have policies to combat forced labor.
